title: Ken Houston's Calendar and Communications Regarding Flock Safety Cameras and ALPRs

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: Ken Houston's Calendar and Communications Regarding Flock Safety Cameras and ALPRs
communications.communication:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Sincerely,

Gabriel Lopez

title: Community ALPR Audit (Bloomington Police Department)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act., I hereby request the following records:

1. Justification Records
All records used to justify adoption of Flock Safety cameras, including but not limited to: staff reports, city council or county board memoranda, crime analysis reports, presentations, vendor proposals, and any other documents that described the purpose, anticipated benefit, or stated rationale for purchasing or leasing the system.

2. Effectiveness and Use Statistics
All statistical reports, internal summaries, use-justification documents, crime-reduction assessments, or after-action reviews evaluating the effectiveness of Flock Safety cameras since installation. This includes any records prepared in connection with contract renewals.

3. Cost and Financial Records
All financial records related to Flock Safety cameras, including invoices, purchase orders, annual subscription payments, installation costs, and maintenance costs from the date of first installation through the present. Include any records showing federal, state, grant, or civil asset forfeiture funding used to offset costs.

4. Camera Inventory and Placement
The current inventory or deployment list of Flock Safety cameras operated by or accessible to this agency, including number of units and general location descriptions. Also include any written policies, criteria, risk assessments, or internal documents used to determine camera placement locations.

5. Contracts and Legal Agreements
Copies of all active and recently expired contracts, amendments, renewals, data-sharing agreements, memoranda of understanding, and scope-of-work documents between this agency and Flock Safety.

6. Data Access and Network Sharing
(a) The Flock "Organization Audit" report showing all searches made within this agency's Flock system from the date of installation through the date of this request.
(b) The Flock "Network Audit" report showing all searches of this agency's Flock data by any outside agency from the date of installation through the date of this request.
(c) A list of all agencies currently authorized to access this agency's Flock data, including any federal agencies.
Please provide these records in their original electronic, machine-readable format (CSV or spreadsheet).

7. Security Incidents and Unauthorized Access
All records documenting any security incidents, unauthorized access, data breaches, hacking events, or unauthorized sharing of data collected by Flock Safety cameras, including any correspondence with or notifications from Flock Safety related to such incidents.

8. Secretary of State Audit
All records, correspondence, or internal communications related to the Illinois Secretary of State's 2025 audit of ALPR data-sharing practices, including any communications with the Secretary of State's office or Flock Safety regarding compliance.

9. Marketing and Sales Materials
All promotional, descriptive, and marketing materials shared with this agency by Flock Safety or any representative thereof prior to or in connection with the purchase or renewal of the Flock Safety system.

10. Communications with Flock Safety
All emails, letters, or other written communications between agency personnel and any @flocksafety.com email address from January 1, 2022, through the date this request is processed.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Brad Bell

communications.communication:
Additional time is needed as it is not practically possible to provide a response within the five working days outlined by FOIA. Therefore, in accordance with Virginia Code § 2.2-3704(B)(4), we will require an additional seven working days beyond the initial five days to respond to your request.

In addition, could you please clarify whether you are looking for just patrol officers, or all officers, i.e., detectives, community outreach, special investigations, recruiting, special operations, etc.

Thank you.

communications.subject: RE: Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: Newport News Va  Police partol  staff roster request as of April22,2026
communications.communication:
Subject: Clarification of FOIA Request – Patrol Officers Only

Good afternoon,

Thank you for your response. To clarify my request:

I am seeking information only for sworn personnel assigned to standard patrol operations within the Newport News Police Department. This includes officers assigned to patrol precincts, patrol beats, and regular patrol field operations, but does not include detectives, community outreach, special investigations, recruiting, special operations, traffic, administrative sworn staff, or any other non‑patrol units.

K‑9 officers should be included only if they are assigned to patrol duties.

Supervisory ranks (sergeants, lieutenants, captains, etc.) and special tactical teams should remain excluded as stated in the original request.

Thank you for the clarification opportunity. Please proceed with the request based on this narrowed scope.
I hope to hear from you soon. 
Take care and be well. 
Thank you for your time and  asstistance. 
Please let me know if you have any more questions or concerns about this request. 
I look forward to hearing from you soon. 

Sincerely,
Cameron Oliver
904-489-9118 
Oliverkidsjax@gmail.com 
(on behalf of Tom Nash)

title: Boston Fair Housing Evaluation RFP  EV00015524

communications.subject: Massachusetts Public Records Law Request: Boston Fair Housing Evaluation RFP  EV00015524
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Massachusetts Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

I am requesting access to specific records related to RFP EV00015524, issued by the Boston Planning Department and associated with a Fair Housing evaluation project, with procurement activity coordinated by Reuben Kantor.

To facilitate a prompt and efficient response, I am requesting the following records for the period June 1, 2025 through the present:

1. The executed contract(s), including any amendments, scope of work, or attachments, resulting from RFP EV00015524.
2. All invoices submitted by the selected vendor(s), along with any corresponding payment or approval records.
3. Any written progress reports, status updates, draft or final deliverables submitted by the vendor(s) to the Boston Planning Department.
4. Meeting agendas, minutes, summaries, or calendar entries documenting meetings between past and present Boston Planning Department staff and the vendor(s) concerning this project.
5. Meeting agendas, minutes, summaries, or calendar entries documenting meetings between past and present Boston Planning Department staff, members of Coalition for a Truly Affordable Boston. Armani White, Boston Tenant Coalition, Kathy Brown,  Lincoln Larmond, Margaret Turner, James Jenning, Matthew Lawlor, Barbara Parmenter, Aisha Miller, Jose Masso, Marvin Martin, Josh Wachman,  Abundant Housing, Elvira Moira, David Baron, Sherry Dong, Inya Ambrogi-Asaza, John Walkey, David Linhart, Travis Lee, L.Goodman, 
6. Email communications to or from Reuben Kantor and other Boston Planning Department staff that reference “EV00015524,” “fair housing,” or the selected vendor’s name, limited to correspondence concerning project status, milestones, deliverables, or performance.

To assist with timely production, I am willing to accept responsive records on a rolling or partial basis as they become available, rather than waiting for the entire request to be fulfilled.

If any portion of this request is denied, please provide the specific statutory exemption(s) relied upon and release all reasonably segregable portions of responsive records.

I also request that, if appropriate, fees be waived as we believe this request is in the public interest, as suggested but not stipulated by the recommendations of the Massachusetts Supervisor of Public Records. RE: Comprehensive FOIA Request – All Records Relating to “Reed v. City of Wynne,” plus Related Matters (2000-Present)

Dear FOIA Custodian:

Pursuant to the Arkansas Freedom of Information Act, Ark. Code Ann. § 25-19-101 et seq., I respectfully request access to, and copies of, the records described below. This request encompasses all responsive records in the possession, custody, or control of the City of Wynne, Cross County, and any of their departments, divisions, boards, commissions, law-enforcement agencies, jail or detention facilities, risk-management offices, insurers, elected officials, employees, consultants, or agents.

1. “Reed v. City of Wynne” (any style, docket, or cause number)  
   a. All pleadings, motions, discovery, transcripts, exhibits, judgments, settlements, and court correspondence.  
   b. All investigative files, offense/incident reports, witness statements, interview recordings, photographs, videos, audio, physical evidence logs, chain-of-custody records, and forensic reports.  
   c. All communication—internal or external—related to the case, including e-mails, letters, memoranda, text messages, voicemail logs, phone records, meeting notes, agendas, or minutes.  
   d. All correspondence with insurers, risk-management entities, defense counsel, plaintiffs’ counsel, expert witnesses, or third-party vendors.

2. Civil or criminal cases, administrative claims, or grievances filed against any City of Wynne or Cross County agency, subdivision, employee, or elected official (including but not limited to the police department, sheriff’s office, jail/detention facility, or city council) from January 1, 2000 to present. Please include:  
   • Style of case; court/docket number; date filed and resolved; claim description; outcome; settlement amounts; indemnity paid; attorney fees; insurance information.  
   • Copies of all pleadings, investigative files, settlements, or releases.  
   • All related communications, as defined above.

3. Meetings and deliberations (2000-present)  
   • All agendas, minutes, recordings, notes, and attendance rosters for:  
     – Wynne City Council (regular, special, or emergency sessions)  
     – Cross County Quorum Court meetings  
     – City of Wynne boards, commissions, or committees  
     – “Behind-the-scenes” or non-public gatherings concerning the matters set forth above, including e-mails arranging such meetings or recapping their substance.

4. Insurance coverage (2000-present)  
   • All policies (liability, law-enforcement liability, public officials’ errors & omissions, excess/umbrella, etc.) applicable to the City of Wynne and Cross County, including declaration pages, limits, exclusions, endorsements, renewals, and cancellations.  
   • All claims files, reservation-of-rights letters, correspondence, and payments regarding “Reed v. City of Wynne” or any other claims listed in Item 2.

5. FOIA logs and correspondence  
   • All prior FOIA requests, subpoena requests, or discovery requests concerning “Reed v. City of Wynne” or the matters described above, with the agency’s responses.  
   • All internal deliberations about responding to such requests.

Format and Delivery  
• Please provide electronic copies (PDF for documents, MP4/AVI for video, WAV/MP3 for audio) via secure download link, e-mail, or USB drive.  
• If records exist only in hard copy, kindly notify me of the total page count and copying cost before duplication.

Exempt or Partially Exempt Material  
If you contend that any portion of the requested records is exempt under Arkansas law:  
1. Identify each withheld record or redacted portion.  
2. Cite the specific statutory exemption.  
3. Release all reasonably segregable non-exempt portions.

Fee Waiver / Cost Estimate  
Disclosure of these records will contribute significantly to public understanding of government operations and accountability. I therefore request a waiver or substantial reduction of duplication fees per Ark. Code Ann. § 25-19-105(d)(3)(A). If anticipated costs exceed \$50, please furnish a written cost estimate before incurring any charge.

Response Time  
Under Ark. Code Ann. § 25-19-105(a)(2)(A), the custodian must provide the records “immediately” or within three working days if they are in active use or storage. Please confirm receipt of this request and advise when and how the responsive records will be made available.

Preservation Notice  
Please preserve all potentially responsive records to prevent alteration, deletion, or destruction during the pendency of this request.

Thank you for your prompt attention to this matter. Should you have any questions, or require clarification to expedite processing, please contact me at [phone] or [e-mail].

Sincerely,



cc:  
• Mayor of Wynne, AR  
• Chief of Police, Wynne Police Department  
• Sheriff, Cross County Sheriff’s Office  
• Warden/Supervisor, Cross County Detention Facility  
• City Attorney, City of Wynne  
• County Attorney / Prosecuting Attorney, Cross County  
• Risk Management / Insurance Carrier(s)  

Enclosures: Copy of Ark. Code Ann. § 25-19-101 et seq. (optional)

Sincerely,

Christopher Reed

title: AI Chatbot Interaction Logs — Executive Office for United States Attorneys

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: AI Chatbot Interaction Logs — Executive Office for United States Attorneys
communications.communication:
Subject: FOIA Request — AI Chatbot Interaction Logs

April 7, 2026

Office of Information Policy
U.S. Department of Justice
441 G Street NW, 6th Floor
Washington, DC 20530
DOJ.OIP.FOIA@usdoj.gov

Dear FOIA Officer,

I am writing on behalf of a team of researchers and journalists from Stanford University, the University of Southern California, KNKX Public Radio and the New York Times. We are studying how government agencies are adopting and using AI tools in their daily work.

We are writing to request records related to the Department of Justice's use of generative AI tools by department employees.

We are requesting the following records:

1. All interaction logs from the OIG internal Azure OpenAI chatbot (DOJ-0195), DEA policy query chatbot (DOJ-0316), FBI policy chatbot (DOJ-0269), ChatGPT, Microsoft Copilot, Google Gemini, Claude, or any other generative AI chatbot tool — including prompts submitted by DOJ headquarters employees and responses generated by the AI — for the period from January 1, 2025 through the date this request is processed. We request these records in their native electronic format where available (e.g., HTML exports, JSON, or CSV).

2. Any policies, guidelines, acceptable use policies, or training materials governing DOJ employee use of generative AI chatbot tools.

3. Any data retention or deletion policies applicable to AI chatbot interaction logs, including any schedules for automatic deletion of interaction data.


We are seeking the substantive content of AI interactions for research purposes. We do not need personally identifying information of individual employees (such as names and email addresses).

DOJ's 2025 AI Use Case Inventory documents 315 AI use cases — up from 241 in 2024 and 15 in 2023 — including multiple component-level GenAI chatbots across OIG, DEA, FBI, EOUSA, and BOP (downloadable at https://www.justice.gov/ai/ai-inventory; see also FedScoop, Feb 11, 2026: https://fedscoop.com/justice-department-artificial-intelligence-ai-surveillance-inventory-predictive-technology-algorithm-bias/). We understand the other agencies under DOJ use chatbots: the Bureau of Prisons has used ChatGPT to "improve the quality of written communication, emails, and fliers" and department-wide Microsoft Copilot deployment is also underway (https://www.justice.gov/media/1426076/dl).

We note that records of this type have been made available in response to public records requests in other jurisdictions. See Cascade PBS, WA City Officials Are Using ChatGPT to Write Government Documents (Aug. 2025), https://www.cascadepbs.org/news/2025/08/wa-city-officials-are-using-chatgpt-to-write-government-documents/; see also Texas State AI Usage — Texas Department of Transportation (TxDOT), MuckRock, https://www.muckrock.com/foi/texas-109/texas-state-ai-usage-texas-department-of-transportation-txdot-196227/#files.

The results of this research will be published in peer-reviewed academic venues and will contribute to public understanding of how public servants interact with AI systems. This request is made by an academic research institution for scholarly, non-commercial purposes; as such, we request a fee waiver or reduction. If a fee waiver is not granted, please inform me of estimated costs before proceeding. I am willing to pay fees up to $20.

I understand that this may be a novel type of records request and I am happy to answer any technical questions. Please feel free to contact me at the email address or phone number below.

Thank you for your time and assistance.

Sincerely,
Alexander Spangher
Postdoctoral Researcher
Stanford University
alexspan@stanford.edu

P.S. To facilitate your response, we note that ChatGPT conversation histories can be exported by individual users via Settings > Data Controls > Export Data, which generates a downloadable ZIP file containing conversations in HTML format. For Claude, export is similar, except via "Settings > Privacy > Export Data". For Microsoft Copilot, interaction histories may be available through the Microsoft 365 admin console, the Purview compliance center, or individual user account settings. We are happy to provide additional technical guidance if that would be helpful.

P.P.S. Under the Federal Records Act, recorded information created or received by a federal agency in connection with public business can qualify as a federal record when it is preserved or appropriate for preservation. 44 U.S.C. § 3301(a)(1)(A). NARA has made clear that chats and other electronic messages used for agency business are likely federal records. NARA Bulletin 2023-02 (Jan. 5, 2023), https://www.archives.gov/records-mgmt/bulletins/2023/2023-02. Consistent with that approach, the Army's August 26, 2025 AI compliance memorandum requires Army system owners to capture and manage AI prompts and AI-generated content and to maintain record material needed to support FOIA responses. See "Army CIO Issues Guidance on AI Compliance With Records Management, FOIA," ExecutiveGov (Sept. 2, 2025), https://www.executivegov.com/articles/army-garciga-application-owners-ai-records-mgmt.

title: Faculty Search Records Request

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act (5 ILCS 140), I hereby request the following public records related to the faculty recruitment and hiring process for the 2026-2027 (current) academic cycle within the Grainger College of Engineering.

This request includes records for tenure-track, non-tenure track, teaching, and Specialized Faculty positions. The specific list of job openings/requisition ID include, but are not limited to:
1. Department of Aerospace Engineering - Open Rank Faculty Search / Requisition ID 1032680 Posted on 10/8/2025
2. Department of Industrial & Enterprise Systems Engineering- Open Rank Faculty Search / Requisition ID 1032898 Posted on 10/13/2025
3. Department of Electrical and Computer Engineering – Open Rank Faculty Search / Requisition ID 1033201 Posted on 11/11/2025
4. Department of Bioengineering - Teaching Faculty (Open Rank) / Requisition ID 1034104 Posted on 12/19/2025
5. The Grainger College of Engineering: Professor - Siebel School of Computing and Data Science / Requisition ID 1035082 Posted on 3/20/2026

Requested Records:

Please note that the request is for any and all interviews which may have occurred between January 1, 2026 and April 25, 2026.

1. Interview Recordings: Copies of any and all video or audio recordings of candidate screening interviews, specifically for candidates who provided written consent for such recordings to be made and maintained, as outlined in the UIUC Recruitment and Hiring Guidelines (Effective January 1, 2026).

2. Faculty Candidate Seminar recordings: Copies of any and all video or audio recordings of faculty candidate seminars, specifically for candidates who provided written consent for such recordings to be made and maintained, as outlined in the UIUC Recruitment and Hiring Guidelines (Effective January 1, 2026), even if the Faculty Candidate Seminar is publically advertised as a Departmental Seminar due to confidentiality requests from the candidate.

3. Interview Notes: Copies of all formal interview notes taken by search committee members (excluding personal "memory aides" not shared with the committee, as defined by Illinois law).

This request specifically EXCLUDES:

1. Evaluation Documents: Search committee evaluation forms, rubrics, ranking sheets, and final reports regarding the candidates for the specified positions.
2. Correspondence: Emails, memoranda, and internal communications regarding the selection, ranking, and final recommendations for these hires.
3. Meeting minutes of the Faculty Hiring Committee.


Privacy and Redactions:

Consistent with Section 7(1)(b) and 7(1)(c) of the Act, I request that you redact sensitive personal information such as Social Security Numbers (SSN), home addresses, personal phone numbers, and any other "unique identifiers" as defined by FOIA.

If you determine that any specific record or portion of a record is exempt from disclosure (e.g., the names of unsuccessful candidates who did not consent), please provide a written justification for each redaction or denial, citing the specific FOIA exemption, and release the remaining non-exempt portions of the records.

Format of Records:
I request that these records be provided in their original electronic format (e.g., PDF, MP4, or Excel) via email or a secure file-sharing link.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within the five (5) business days required by the Act.

Sincerely,

Joseph Sentien Sief

communications.communication:

Public records requests are processed in the order that they are received. Processing time may be lengthened due to the nature and extent of the request. title: Flock Event Logs

communications.subject: Georgia Open Records Act Request: Flock Event Logs
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Georgia Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. All audit logs, access logs, or activity records generated by or within the Flock Safety platform reflecting any activity by Lawrenceville Police Department personnel, from June 4, 2025 through the date this request is processed.
2. All records reflecting the granting, approval, configuration, or receipt of access to any Flock Safety camera network shared by another agency with Lawrenceville Police Department, including but not limited to any communications, system notifications, or authorization records related to any such network share.
3. Any written policies, procedures, or guidance governing Lawrenceville Police Department personnel's use of camera feeds received via the Flock Safety network sharing system, including any restrictions on accessing networks shared by other agencies.
For purposes of this request, "Flock Safety platform" includes any Flock Safety product, application, dashboard, or interface, including but not limited to Flock OS, Flock's law enforcement portal, and any associated livestream or video access tools.
I request that responsive records be provided in electronic format. If any portion of this request is denied, please provide a written explanation citing the specific statutory exemption(s) relied upon for each withheld record.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Jason Hunyar

title: Flock Nova Platform Audit Logs

communications.subject: Georgia Open Records Act Request: Flock Nova Platform Audit Logs
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Georgia Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. Flock Nova Audit Logs
All audit logs, access logs, event logs, and activity records generated within the Flock Safety platform reflecting use of, or access to, the Nova feature by any user associated with the Dunwoody PD organization, for the period June 30, 2025 through the date of this request.
For context, Dunwoody PD's Nova feature was enabled on or about June 30, 2025 (Flock audit event ID: 869ade3e-85d4-50b2-b426-74cae52b32eb), as reflected in existing audit records already in the city's possession.
Specifically, I request records sufficient to identify:
a. All Nova searches, queries, or lookups conducted by any Dunwoody PD user or any Flock Safety employee operating under Dunwoody credentials, including the initiating user, timestamp, search parameters, and any associated case number or stated purpose;
b. All records reflecting the results returned by Nova queries, to the extent retained by the city;
c. Any policies, procedures, training materials, or usage guidelines provided to Dunwoody PD in connection with Nova access.
2. Responsive Format
I request that records be produced in their native digital format (CSV, XLSX, JSON, or equivalent) where available, rather than printed or converted to PDF. If records exist only in a non-exportable format, please describe the format and available export options before producing.
If any costs are anticipated, please provide an itemized cost estimate — broken down by staff time, hourly rate, and any per-page or processing fees — prior to beginning production, as required by O.C.G.A. § 50-18-71.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Jason Hunyar

title: Public Data Request – September 30, 2023 Fatal Crash (Irma & Wilma Miller) – Fillmore County (Minnesota Bureau Of Criminal Apprehension St. Paul, Minnesota, Usa)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Minnesota Government Data Practices Act, I hereby request the following records:

Dear Custodian of Records,

Pursuant to the Minnesota Government Data Practices Act (Minn. Stat. Ch. 13), I respectfully request access to the following multimedia records related to a fatal motor vehicle collision involving an Amish buggy and a Toyota 4Runner that occurred on September 30, 2023, at approximately 8:25 AM – 9:30 AM at County Road 1 near County Road 102, Sumner Township, Fillmore County, Minnesota, resulting in the deaths of Irma Miller and Wilma Miller.

Example Requested Records:

911 Audio
911 audio of the call(s) placed by passersby or involved parties on September 30, 2023, at approximately 8:25 AM – 8:45 AM, reporting a motor vehicle collision involving an Amish buggy at County Road 1 near County Road 102, Fillmore County, Minnesota. (Caller name unknown)
Body-Worn Camera – Initial Response
Body-worn camera video and audio from the first responding officer(s) at County Road 1 near County Road 102, documenting arrival, initial scene assessment, and confirmation of deceased victim(s), including Irma Miller and Wilma Miller.

Body-Worn Camera – Specific Event (Witness Statement)
Body-worn camera video and audio capturing the initial statement provided by Sarah Peterson at approximately 8:30 AM – 9:30 AM on September 30, 2023, at the crash scene, including the identification of herself as the driver of the Toyota 4Runner.

In-Car Dash Camera
In-car dash camera video and audio capturing the arrival, positioning, and on-scene activity of Fillmore County Sheriff’s Office vehicles at approximately 8:25 AM on September 30, 2023, at County Road 1 near County Road 102, including the recorded interactions at the scene.

Body-Worn Camera – Evidence Collection
Body-worn camera video and audio documenting evidence collection at the scene on September 30, 2023, including documentation of the Amish buggy wreckage, Toyota 4Runner damage, roadway debris, and the recovered items or suspected contraband.

Crime Scene Photographs
Crime scene photographs taken at County Road 1 and County Road 102 depict the impact point, final resting positions of the Amish buggy and Toyota 4Runner, and the deceased victims as documented at the scene.

Evidence Processing Photographs
Evidence processing photographs associated with Case Nos. 23-CR-24-106 and 23-CR-24-107, specifically including:
Damage to the Toyota 4Runner
The destroyed Amish buggy
the digital evidence images, such as screenshots or photographs of text messages recovered from suspect mobile devices

Body-Worn Camera / Dash Camera – Arrest or Post-Investigation Contact
Body-worn camera/ dashcam/MVR video and audio documenting the arrest, charging contact, or surrender/processing of Samantha Jo Peterson and Sarah Beth Peterson following the investigation (approximately February 2024), including the custody transfer or booking-related footage if available.

Interrogation Room Recordings
Full interview room video and audio recording(s) of the custodial or non-custodial interviews of Samantha Jo Peterson and Sarah Beth Peterson conducted by the Fillmore County Sheriff’s Office or the Minnesota Bureau of Criminal Apprehension between September 30, 2023, and February 2024.

Witness Interviews – Rochester Hy-Vee Employees
Full video and audio recording(s) of witness interviews conducted with coworkers at the Rochester Hy-Vee by the Fillmore County Sheriff’s Office following the incident, specifically regarding the statements made by the suspects about the crash or related events.

CCTV / surveillance video from Hy-Vee (Rochester, MN) for the period 7:00 AM – 10:00 AM on September 30, 2023, depicting the Peterson twins' arrival/departure or the interactions with coworkers immediately following the crash.

Incident Details:

Incident Numbers: 23-CR-24-106 and 23-CR-24-107
Victim(s): Irma Miller, Wilma Miller
Subject(s): Sarah Peterson, Samantha Jo Peterson, Sarah Beth Peterson 
Date of Incident: September 30, 2023
Time of Incident: Approximately 8:25 AM – 9:30 AM
Location of Incident: County Road 1 near County Road 102, Sumner Township, Fillmore County, Minnesota

This request seeks native digital copies of the above-listed multimedia records as maintained by your agency. I am not requesting written reports, CAD logs, transcripts, or administrative summaries at this time.

If any portion of this request is denied, please cite the specific statutory exemption for each denial and release all reasonably segregable portions of responsive records.

Please provide an itemized cost estimate prior to processing. I prefer electronic delivery via secure download link or USB drive.

If your agency does not maintain any portion of these records, please forward this request to the appropriate custodian or advise me accordingly.

Thank you for your time and assistance.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Danielle D.

communications.subject: FW: Georgia Open Records Act Request: TANF sanction statistics
communications.communication:
Good Morning,

Please note the Department of Human Services has a designated Open Records Officer and Inbox. All Open Record requests should be directed to the Open Records inbox, and the timing for response will begin upon the Open Record Officer’s receipt of such request. The below request was forwarded to the Open Records inbox on 04/22/2026.

Your request has been forwarded for review. This request will be reviewed to preliminarily determine whether any DHS records exist that would be responsive and subject to disclosure. If a search is possible and responsive records subject to disclosure do exist, and if such records are available within three business days (04/27/2026) , DHS anticipates providing you with such records or an estimate of charges for you to confirm or reject. If a search is possible and responsive records subject to disclosure do exist, and if such records are unavailable within three business days, DHS anticipates providing you with a timeline and an estimate of charges for you to confirm or reject. If responsive records do not exist or are not subject to disclosure, DHS anticipates notifying you accordingly. In the event clarification is required to proceed with either search or production of responsive records or development of a timeline and an estimate of charges, we may reach out to you to obtain clarification about your request.
Please be advised that, because you have submitted an open records request, DHS will only search for existing records that would be responsive to your request. DHS is not required to provide confidential records and records otherwise exempt from disclosure under Georgia’s Open Records Act in response to a request for records made pursuant to Georgia’s Open Records Act. See O.C.G.A. §§ 50-18-71(a), 50-18-72(a). DHS is not required to prepare new reports, summaries, or compilations not in existence at the time of requests for records made pursuant to Georgia’s Open Records Act. See O.C.G.A. § 50-18-71(j). Furthermore, DHS may impose a reasonable charge for the search, retrieval, redaction, and production or copying costs for the production of records in response to an open records request at an amount permitted by O.C.G.A. § 50-18-71, and whenever any person who has requested to inspect or copy a public record has not paid the cost for search, retrieval, redaction, or copying of such records when such charges have been lawfully incurred, DHS may require prepayment for compliance with all future requests for production of records from that person until the costs for the prior production of records have been paid. See O.C.G.A. §§ 50-18-71(c) & d).
